summary: Bright, crisp audience recording captures a solid ’86 show with standout Friend of the Devil, Cassidy and an energetic second set.
review: Both reviewers praise this June 30‑1986 pull as one of the best audience tapes of the year, noting its bright, full‑range sound and clear front‑of‑house placement. The performance shines on Friend of the Devil, Cassidy, Mighty Quinn (encore) and a lively second set featuring Bertha → Women, Smokestack Lightning into He’s Gone, while Jerry’s vocals are described as “halfway decent.”

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed reviews give an average of 3.5 stars but both highlight strong musical moments and a high‑quality audience recording, pushing the overall rating toward the upper‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bright and crisp front‑of‑house capture with full range and fat sound; a bit too dynamic/tinny for some listeners but overall very clear.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set praised for its momentum – a jumpy Bertha → Women opener, smooth transition from Smokestack Lightning into He's Gone, and an uplifting Mighty Quinn encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as halfway decent; guitar work not specifically noted.
